fixes_verified.note: Wave 3 strings.xml conflict resolved. Task 7 owns strings.xml in files-to-modify (creates all keys for Phase 1). Tasks 8 and 9 list strings.xml only in files-to-read with explicit 'из Task 7' annotation. Description of Task 7 explicitly states 'strings.xml создаётся в этой задаче как единственном месте' and 'Задачи 8 и 9 только читают strings.xml, не модифицируют'. No more parallel-write conflict.

Wave 3 MainActivity.kt conflict resolved. Task 10 moved to new Wave 4 (heading 'Wave 4 (зависит от Wave 3 — 7, 8, 9)'). Task 7 creates MainActivity.kt (stub setContent). Task 10 updates setContent with SanctumApp wiring — in a later wave, so sequencing is correct. Task 7 description notes 'setContent со stub-заглушкой до Wave 4' and Task 10 description notes 'Task 7 оставил stub'.

Dependencies section (New packages + Gradle plugins) no longer lists kotlinx-serialization-json or kotlin.plugin.serialization. T-R5 risk is consistent. Minor residue: Decision T2 version-catalog list still mentions kotlinx-serialization-json=1.7.3 as an available pinned version from architecture.md, but it is not actually added to Dependencies. Not a contradiction — T2 documents the version catalog, Dependencies documents what is included. Fix intent met.

Task descriptions trimmed. Counts: Task 3 ~4 sentences (WHAT + reference to code-research + Decisions), Task 4 ~2, Task 5 ~2, Task 6 ~3, Task 7 ~5 (still within 5-sentence cap; bootstrap + manifest + strings.xml ownership + tone anchor + read-only note for 8/9), Task 8 ~4, Task 9 ~4. All point to Decisions (T5, T6, T8, T9, T10, T11) and code-research.md sections instead of inlining mechanics. Kotlin snippets and widget-level detail removed from task descriptions.

No line-number references remain inside Implementation Tasks descriptions (scan for 'line', ':NNN', 'строка' in descriptions — 0 hits). All surgical coordinates delegated to 'code-research.md § N' anchors. Observation: Architecture § What-we're-building and Decisions T6/T10 still carry line numbers (lines 123–125, 32/105/134–137, 342, 276, 238–241). These were NOT in the R1 scope (R1 finding targeted 'tech-spec task descriptions'). Flagging as an observation under new_issues for author awareness, not as a re-violation.

Task 14 description no longer re-specifies full AC-14 protocol. It now references 'AC-14 protocol из Agent Verification Plan' and states the gate succinctly ('3 прогона Gallery + 3 прогона Phase 1, медианы, гейт median(S)/median(G) ≤ 1.5'). The detailed 4-step protocol (cold-prefill between runs, Gi/Si values, numeric vs subjective gate) lives only in Agent Verification Plan. Acceptable cross-reference.
Audit Wave (Tasks 11, 12, 13) all have 'Reviewers: none' — intentional design: audit tasks ARE the reviewers themselves, so no secondary review loop. Consistent.
Final Wave contains Task 14 with skill=pre-deploy-qa. Required QA present.
14 tasks total (Wave 0: 2, Wave 1: 3, Wave 2: 1, Wave 3: 3, Wave 4: 1, Audit: 3, Final: 1). Under the 15-task threshold. No split needed.

new_issues.issue: Architecture § 'What we're building/modifying' (bullets on DownloadRepository.kt and DownloadWorker.kt) and Decisions T6/T10 still carry Gallery source line numbers (e.g. 'lines 123–125', ':342', ':238-241'). Not flagged in R1 but worth noting: this is the same kind of implementation coupling that was pushed out of task descriptions — in principle it belongs in code-research.md too. Not blocking.
new_issues.fix: Optional: replace remaining line-numbers in Architecture/Decisions with 'see code-research.md § N' anchors (§1, §2, §10.3, §4 already exist). Keeps tech-spec stable against Gallery reshuffles.

new_issues.issue: Task 1 lists reviewer 'infrastructure-reviewer' and Task 2 same. Not in the standard reviewer catalog (code-reviewer / security-auditor / test-reviewer / skill-checker / prompt-reviewer). Also present across other infra tasks was flagged in R1 scope only by task-quality skills section — this is a minor catalog-mismatch note.
new_issues.fix: Either add 'infrastructure-reviewer' to the reviewers catalog in skills-and-reviewers.md, or rename to an existing reviewer (e.g. code-reviewer already covers Gradle/config quality). Not blocking R2.

summary: All six R1 findings verified fixed. No new critical or major violations introduced by the R2 edits. Wave-level merge-conflict risk eliminated (strings.xml fully owned by Task 7; MainActivity.kt sequenced Wave 3 -> Wave 4). Task descriptions now consistently operate at WHAT/WHY level with anchors to Decisions and code-research.md. T-R5 dependency contradiction resolved. Task 14 references AVP instead of duplicating the protocol. Two minor observations raised (residual line-numbers in Architecture/Decisions; non-catalog 'infrastructure-reviewer' mention) — informational only, do not block approval.
